content_id:
Metodologi al-Baghawi dalam Syarh al-Sunnah mencerminkan pembentukannya dalam tradisi hukum Syafi'i dan ilmu-ilmu hadis zamannya. Pendekatannya terhadap komentar bersifat aksesibel dan praktis, ditulis untuk para ulama dan pelajar serius yang perlu memahami baik apa makna hadis-hadis maupun bagaimana hadis-hadis itu berfungsi dalam penalaran hukum Islam.

Untuk setiap hadis atau sekelompok hadis yang berkaitan, al-Baghawi umumnya memulai dengan menyajikan teks beserta sanadnya. Beliau mengidentifikasi koleksi sumber tempat hadis itu diambil, umumnya mengutip dari koleksi-koleksi kanonik utama: Shahih al-Bukhari, Shahih Muslim, dan keempat kitab Sunan. Terkadang beliau mencatat sanad-sanad tambahan atau riwayat-riwayat pendukung ketika hal ini relevan.

Komentar-komentar penjelasan al-Baghawi bersifat praktis dan terfokus. Beliau menjelaskan kosakata yang tidak biasa atau bersifat teknis dalam teks hadis, mengandalkan penguasaannya atas tradisi linguistik Arab untuk mengklarifikasi makna-makna yang mungkin tidak jelas bagi pembacanya. Beliau kemudian mengidentifikasi pelajaran-pelajaran hukum atau etis utama dari hadis tersebut, biasanya menyajikan posisi-posisi mazhab hukum utama atas masalah-masalah yang muncul.

Perlakuannya terhadap perselisihan hukum antar-mazhab mencerminkan pembentukannya dalam mazhab Syafi'i tetapi umumnya seimbang dalam penyajian. Beliau biasanya menyajikan posisi Syafi'i beserta dalilnya, kemudian mencatat posisi-posisi Hanafi dan Maliki beserta landasannya, terkadang menambahkan penilaian singkat tentang kekuatan komparatif dalil-dalil tersebut. Nadanya bersifat ilmiah dan tidak polemik, sesuai dengan penerimaan luas yang didapat karya ini di seluruh empat mazhab utama.

Pendekatan al-Baghawi terhadap kontradiksi yang tampak antara hadis-hadis mengikuti metodologi standar: mencari rekonsiliasi (jam') yang memungkinkan kedua riwayat diterapkan dalam konteks yang sesuai, sebelum menggunakan perangkat nasakh atau tarjih ketika rekonsiliasi tidak memungkinkan. Pembahasan beliau tentang strategi rekonsiliasi ini memberikan pengantar praktis terhadap metodologi penanganan dalil yang saling bertentangan.

Kitab ini ditulis dalam prosa Arab yang jelas dan mudah dibaca, yang tetap terbaca sepanjang berabad-abad, salah satu alasan mengapa karya ini terus diajarkan dan dijadikan rujukan jauh setelah wafatnya al-Baghawi.
content_en:
Al-Baghawi's methodology in Sharh as-Sunnah reflects his training in both the Shafi'i legal tradition and the hadith sciences of his era. His approach to the commentary is accessible and practical — he writes for scholars and serious students who need to understand both what hadiths mean and how they function in Islamic legal reasoning.

For each hadith or group of related hadiths, al-Baghawi typically begins by presenting the text with its chain of transmission. He identifies the source collection from which he draws the hadith, generally citing from the major canonical collections: Sahih al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, and the four Sunan. He sometimes notes additional chains or supporting narrations when these are relevant.

Al-Baghawi's explanatory comments are practical and focused. He explains unusual or technical vocabulary in the hadith text, drawing on his command of the Arabic linguistic tradition to clarify meanings that might be obscure to his readers. He then identifies the main legal or ethical lessons of the hadith, typically presenting the positions of the major legal schools on the issues raised.

His treatment of legal disputes between the schools reflects his Shafi'i formation but is generally balanced in presentation. He typically presents the Shafi'i position with its evidence, then notes the Hanafi and Maliki positions and their bases, sometimes adding a brief evaluation of the comparative strength of the evidence. His tone is scholarly and non-polemical, consistent with the broad acceptance the work received across all four major schools.

Al-Baghawi's approach to apparent contradictions between hadiths follows the standard methodology: seeking a reconciliation (jam') that allows both narrations to be applied in their appropriate contexts, before resorting to the tools of abrogation (naskh) or preferential selection (tarjih) when reconciliation is not possible. His discussions of these reconciliation strategies provide a practical introduction to the methodology of dealing with conflicting evidence.

The work is written in clear, accessible Arabic prose that remains readable across the centuries — one of the reasons it continued to be taught and referenced long after al-Baghawi's death.
content_ar: Al-Baghawi's methodology in Sharh as-السنة reflects his training in both the الشافعي legal التراث and the الحديث sciences of his era. His approach to the commentary is accessible and practical — he writes for العلماء and serious students who need لفهم both what hadiths mean and how they function in Islamic legal reasoning. For each الحديث or group of related hadiths, al-Baghawi typically begins by presenting the text with its chain of transmission. He identifies the source collection from which he draws the الحديث, generally citing from the major canonical collections: صحيح البخاري, صحيح مسلم, and the four Sunan. He sometimes notes additional chains or supporting narrations عندما these are relevant. Al-Baghawi's explanatory comments are practical and focused. He explains unusual or technical vocabulary in the الحديث text, drawing on his command of the Arabic linguistic التراث to clarify meanings that might be obscure to his readers. He then identifies the الرئيسي legal or ethical lessons of the الحديث, typically presenting the positions of the major legal schools on the issues raised. His treatment of legal disputes between the schools reflects his الشافعي formation but is generally balanced in presentation. He typically presents the الشافعي position with its evidence, then notes the الحنفي and المالكي positions and their bases, sometimes adding a brief evaluation of the comparative القوة of the evidence. His tone is العلمي and non-polemical, consistent with the broad acceptance the work received across all four major schools. Al-Baghawi's approach to apparent contradictions between hadiths follows the standard methodology: seeking a reconciliation (jam') that allows both narrations to be applied in their appropriate contexts, قبل resorting to the tools of abrogation (naskh) or preferential selection (tarjih) عندما reconciliation is not possible. His discussions of these reconciliation strategies provide a practical introduction to the methodology of dealing with conflicting evidence. The work is written in clear, accessible Arabic prose that remains readable across the centuries — one of the reasons it continued to be taught and referenced long بعد al-Baghawi's الموت.
